quickAdd.label: Quick add proxy
quickAdd.placeholder:
Paste proxies here.
Supported formats:
http://user:pass@host:port
host:port:user:pass
user:pass:host:port
host port user pass
IP: host\nHTTP Port: 8080\nUsername: user\nPassword: pass
quickAdd.description: Paste one or many proxies. BugLogin will auto-parse common formats and import every valid entry.

validation.noValid: No valid proxies found. {{count}} entries need manual review.

fingerprint.crossOsWarning: Spoofing fingerprint for a different operating system is less reliable because it is impossible to perfectly mimic all underlying components. Use with caution.
fingerprint.crossOsLimitations: Cross-OS fingerprinting has limitations. System-level APIs may still reflect your actual operating system, and some features may have degraded performance.
fingerprint.generateRandomOnLaunch: Generate random fingerprint on every launch
fingerprint.generateRandomDescription: When enabled, a new fingerprint will be generated each time the browser is launched.
fingerprint.generateRandomDescriptionAuto: When enabled, a new fingerprint will be generated each time the browser is launched. The generated fingerprint is saved for reference.
fingerprint.autoLocationDescription: Automatically configure location information based on proxy configuration or your connection if no proxy provided
fingerprint.editingDisabledRunning: Fingerprint editing is disabled because the profile is currently running. Stop the profile to make changes.
fingerprint.editingDisabledRandomized: Fingerprint editing is disabled because random fingerprint generation is enabled. Disable the option above to manually edit the fingerprint configuration.
fingerprint.advancedWarning: Warning: Only edit these parameters if you know what you're doing. Incorrect values may break websites, make them detect you, and lead to hard-to-debug bugs.
fingerprint.basicWarning: Warning: Only edit these parameters if you know what you're doing.
fingerprint.timezoneGeolocationDescription: These values override the browser's timezone and geolocation APIs.
fingerprint.canvasNoiseSeedDescription: This seed is used to generate a consistent but unique canvas fingerprint. Each profile should have a different seed.

warnings.windowResizeDescription: Changing browser window dimensions may increase the chance of website detection that browser information is spoofed.
warnings.windowResizeCamoufoxDescription: Camoufox locks the viewport to the spoofed screen dimensions for anti-fingerprinting. Resizing the window may cause cropped or grey areas. This is expected behavior.

syncAll.title: Enable Sync for Existing Items

ui.localModeEnabled: Local/self-host mode is active. Workspace governance actions run locally; Stripe/S3 features still require service configuration.
ui.readOnlyHint: Current account is read-only in this area. Use an Owner/Admin role to apply changes.
ui.userPermissionDescription: Manage invites, role assignment, share policy, and role-based capability toggles for the active workspace.
ui.userPermissionOverviewDescription: Review role distribution and permission posture before changing membership or sharing policy.
ui.userPermissionGuidanceDescription: Invite as Member/Viewer first, then elevate role after ownership and responsibility are confirmed.
ui.memberAccessDescription: Manage role lifecycle, revoke access, and monitor invite throughput for the active workspace.
ui.searchMembersPlaceholder: Search by email or user ID...
ui.searchSharesPlaceholder: Search resource ID or recipient email...
ui.noRowsInPage: No rows on this page. Change filters or page.
ui.policyChecklistDescription: Apply these controls before sharing resources across operators or partner workspaces.
ui.policyChecklistItem1: Grant only least-privileged access for each role or recipient.
ui.policyChecklistItem2: Use explicit revocation immediately when ownership context changes.
ui.policyChecklistItem3: Review active shares and invite status before billing or seat audits.
ui.serviceAuthDescription: Controls login, invite acceptance, and session verification.
ui.serviceStripeDescription: Handles subscription lifecycle, invoices, and payment webhooks.
ui.serviceSyncDescription: Protects backup and restore flows across devices.
ui.healthDescription: Keep release-critical services visible before switching environments.
ui.entitlementDescription: Monitor effective runtime limits and retention policies.
ui.latestAuditDescription: Latest sensitive actions captured by the control-plane.
ui.operationQueueDescription: Action-first queue for risky, overdue, or near-capacity workspaces requiring operator intervention.
ui.operationQueueEmpty: No workspace currently requires urgent intervention.
ui.reasonHighRisk: Risk posture is high and needs immediate triage.
ui.reasonPastDue: Subscription is past due and may impact entitlement.
ui.reasonCapacity: Storage or proxy usage is nearing configured limits.
ui.reasonShareAndInvite: Review sharing and invite posture before scale-up.
ui.createWorkspaceDescription: Provision a new isolated workspace and start governance immediately.
ui.shareDescription: Grant access to workspace resources with explicit revoke control.
ui.systemGuidanceItem1: Confirm auth and Stripe are ready before enabling paid seat changes.
ui.systemGuidanceItem2: Keep S3 sync healthy before triggering large backup/restore operations.
ui.systemGuidanceItem3: When a service is pending config, keep app startup non-blocking and surface clear warnings.

workspaceUsers.title: Workspace & Users
workspaceUsers.description: Manage workspaces, seat usage, and membership roles.

billingEntitlement.title: Billing & Entitlement
billingEntitlement.description: Track subscription lifecycle, grace transitions, and read-only fallback.

coupon.title: Coupon Governance
coupon.description: Control internal discounts with allowlist, expiry, and redemption caps.

tiktokCookies.title: TikTok Cookie Operations
tiktokCookies.description: Push and maintain TikTok cookies for BugIdea endpoints.

audit.title: Audit Logs
audit.description: Review sensitive actions with actor, reason, and outcome details.

systemConfig.title: System Config
systemConfig.description: Monitor auth, Stripe, and S3 readiness without blocking app startup.

analytics.title: Operational & Revenue Analytics
analytics.description: Observe platform health and revenue snapshots in one workspace.

proxyPage.title: Proxy Center
proxyPage.description: Keep proxies, VPNs, and quick network operations in a wider workspace instead of a cramped modal.
proxyPage.empty: No proxies yet. Open the manager to import or quick-add inventory.
proxyPage.quickActionsDescription: Use the full manager when you need batch imports, validation, or VPN editing.

settingsPage.title: Settings Hub
settingsPage.description: Keep primary app controls accessible from a full page, then jump into the detailed editor only when needed.
settingsPage.layoutNoteDescription: This shell is designed to move heavy workflows out of modal stacks and into dedicated spaces.

integrationsPage.title: Integrations Hub
integrationsPage.description: Monitor local API and MCP access from a page view, then open the full control center when you need config details.
integrationsPage.nextStepDescription: Use this area as the command surface for external tools and assistants that connect to BugLogin.

webPortal.movedDescription: This function is now managed on the web portal. Desktop remains focused on runtime operations.
webPortal.superAdminDescription: Platform governance has moved to the web Super Admin Portal.
webPortal.workspaceOwnerDescription: Workspace governance has moved to the web Workspace Owner Portal.
webPortal.billingDescription: Billing management is now web-first and handled in the portal.
webPortal.pricingDescription: Pricing and checkout are now handled on the web portal.

topbar.systemReadyDescription: Auth, Stripe, and Sync are ready.

states.bootDescription: Preparing workspace shell and account context...
states.postLoginDescription: Loading workspace data and access context...
states.sectionSwitchDescription: Preparing data and interface for this workspace page...

pricingPage.description: Compare plans, evaluate value, and choose the right subscription path before managing invoices.
pricingPage.heroTitle: Choose the right plan for the current workspace
pricingPage.heroTitleSubscribed: Optimize your current subscription for growth
pricingPage.heroDescription: Compare plan limits, pick the right tier for the active workspace, then continue in Billing to activate it.
pricingPage.planFootnote: Tax and local regulations may apply.
pricingPage.compareDescription: Quickly compare limits and support levels before making your decision.
pricingPage.comparisonNote: Need custom security and compliance? Contact us for enterprise onboarding.
pricingPage.enterpriseLine: For teams that need custom limits, enterprise security, and dedicated support.
pricingPage.faq1Q: Can I upgrade or downgrade later?
pricingPage.faq1A: Yes. You can switch plans at any time from Billing Management and changes apply to the next billing cycle.
pricingPage.faq2A: Yearly mode applies a discounted annual price while keeping the same feature limits and support level.
pricingPage.faq3Q: Do I need Stripe ready before selecting a plan?
pricingPage.faq3A: No. You can pre-select plans now and activate full checkout as soon as Stripe is configured.
pricingPage.ctaTitle: Need help choosing the right plan?
pricingPage.ctaDescription: Start with a recommended plan, then complete payment through dedicated Checkout/Coupon/License pages.
pricingPage.paymentFlowStripe: Stripe checkout + coupon/license on dedicated payment pages
pricingPage.paymentFlowSelfHost: Self-host checkout + coupon/license on dedicated payment pages
pricingPage.memberUpgradeBlocked: Only owner/admin can change plans for this workspace.
pricingPage.memberReadonlyDescription: You only have permission to view the current workspace plan.
pricingPage.memberReadonlyHint: Contact workspace owner/admin to upgrade or change billing.
pricingPage.downgradeDisabled: This account has multiple workspaces, so only upgrades are self-serve. Downgrades require manual handling.
pricingPage.downgradeDisabledHint: This account has multiple workspaces, so self-service only supports upgrades. Downgrades are handled manually in Billing.
pricingPage.planActionDescription: You selected {{plan}}. Choose where to apply this plan.
pricingPage.planActionDescriptionFallback: Choose where to apply this plan before checkout.
pricingPage.actionUpgradeCurrentDescription: Apply to workspace {{workspace}} and continue to Checkout page.
pricingPage.actionCreateWorkspaceTitle: Create a new workspace with this plan
pricingPage.actionCreateWorkspaceDescription: Create a new team workspace, then continue with payment/coupon/license flow.
pricingPage.workspaceCreateRequiresSync: Configure Sync server before creating a workspace from pricing flow.

billingPage.title: Pricing & Billing
billingPage.description: Manage plans, usage, coupons, and payment posture with business-grade operational visibility.
billingPage.managementPageDescription: Track billing runtime, usage, and invoices, then route payment actions to dedicated pages.
billingPage.checkoutPageDescription: Payment page for the current workspace plan.
billingPage.couponPageDescription: Coupon page for the current workspace after selecting a plan from Pricing.
billingPage.licensePageDescription: License claim page for the current workspace after selecting a plan from Pricing.
billingPage.pricingDescription: Choose billing cycle and tier before enabling production checkout.
billingPage.pricingCurrentPlanHint: You are currently subscribed to this plan.
billingPage.couponDescription: Apply internal discount codes to control rollout and internal support operations.
billingPage.couponQueued: Coupon is ready in self-host flow.
billingPage.couponQueuedDescription: Stripe is not configured, so this coupon is applied through internal self-host billing.
billingPage.couponAppliedDescription: Discount has been attached to the current billing flow.
billingPage.couponValidationUnavailable: Sync server or token is not ready for coupon validation.
billingPage.couponNotEligible: Coupon is invalid or not eligible for this workspace.
billingPage.couponLocalOnly: In local mode, only FREE100/LOCAL100/BUG100 are supported for checkout simulation.
billingPage.workspaceRequiredForBilling: Select a workspace before changing plans.
billingPage.pendingPlanStripeConfigIncomplete: Stripe setup is incomplete. Finish publishable key and billing URL to continue checkout.
billingPage.pendingPlanCouponApplied: Coupon applied. You can confirm and activate this plan.
billingPage.pendingPlanSelfHostedReady: Self-host checkout is ready. Pay now, or apply coupon/license to activate.
billingPage.pendingPlanDirectActivationReady: A 100% coupon or valid license activates directly.
billingPage.pendingProrationCredit: Applied ${{amount}} credit from {{days}} remaining days on the current plan.
billingPage.pendingPlanNeedsPayment: Stripe payment or a valid coupon is required before activation.
billingPage.checkoutUrlMissing: Stripe billing URL is missing in app settings.
billingPage.checkoutStartRequired: Open Stripe Checkout first, then verify after payment.
billingPage.stripeConfigIncompleteDescription: A Stripe key or billing URL is present, but both values are required. Complete the setup to avoid self-host bypass.
billingPage.stripeRequiredForCheckout: Stripe is not ready. Direct paid checkout is blocked until Stripe is configured.
billingPage.stripeRequiredForPaidCoupon: Coupon is valid but cannot activate yet.
billingPage.stripeRequiredForPaidCouponDescription: Coupon discount is below 100%. Configure Stripe to pay the remaining amount.
billingPage.paymentOrCouponRequired: Stripe payment or a valid coupon is required to change plan.
billingPage.planActivatedDescription: Plan {{plan}} has been applied to the current workspace.
billingPage.paymentPendingVerification: Payment for the selected plan has not been verified yet.
billingPage.checkoutSectionSelfHostHint: Self-host mode is active. You can pay now directly, or move through coupon/license.
billingPage.couponInlineHint: Apply a coupon code and update the amount due immediately.
billingPage.licenseInlineHint: Use a valid license to activate directly without Stripe Checkout.
billingPage.licenseDisclosureClosed: Have a license key? Expand this section to activate directly.
billingPage.checkoutFlowCouponApplied: Coupon applied. Continue to checkout confirmation.
billingPage.checkoutFlowSelfHostedSuccess: Self-host payment completed and plan activation requested.
billingPage.checkoutFlowStripeOpened: Stripe Checkout opened. Complete payment and return to verify.
billingPage.checkoutCancelledDescription: You can return to pricing or open checkout again.
billingPage.checkoutReturnSuccessDescription: BugLogin will reopen to verify the payment and activate the plan.
billingPage.checkoutReturnCancelDescription: BugLogin will reopen so you can continue with pricing or retry checkout.
billingPage.checkoutReturnSecondary: If the app does not open automatically, use the button below.
billingPage.controlServerUnavailable: Unable to reach the billing server.
billingPage.controlServerUnavailableDescription: Check `sync_server_url`, the service port, and the buglogin-sync process status.
billingPage.workspaceCheckoutContextExpired: This billing workspace no longer exists. Return to Pricing and choose the plan again from the current workspace.
billingPage.nextRenewalUnknown: Next renewal date is not available yet.
billingPage.autoRenewalSelfHosted: Auto-renew depends on current self-host configuration.
billingPage.autoRenewalDisabledAtPeriodEnd: Auto-renew is off. The plan will end at the close of the current cycle.
billingPage.subscriptionLifecycleDescription: You can stop renewal at period end or end the paid plan immediately.
billingPage.subscriptionScheduledSummary: This plan will end at the close of the current cycle: {{date}}.
billingPage.subscriptionCancelScheduledDescription: The workspace keeps the current plan until the expiry date.
billingPage.subscriptionCancelledImmediatelyDescript: The workspace has been moved back to the default plan now.
billingPage.subscriptionReactivatedDescription: The current plan will renew as normal again.
billingPage.subscriptionUpdateFailed: Unable to update the subscription state.
billingPage.addonUnavailableOnFree: Add-ons are available after selecting a paid plan.
billingPage.paymentOrderSummaryNoPlan: No selected plan to proceed with checkout.
billingPage.paymentClaimDescription: Select a plan first, then claim it for this workspace via coupon or license key.
billingPage.paymentSelfHostedDescription: Self-host checkout is active. Select a pending plan and pay now, or activate with a coupon/license.
billingPage.selectPlanBeforeClaim: Select a plan from Pricing before applying coupon or license.
billingPage.licenseHint: Use an admin-issued license key for plan {{code}}.
billingPage.licenseInvalidDescription: Use a valid key for the selected plan, for example: {{sample}}
billingPage.licenseValidationUnavailable: Unable to validate license from control-plane server.
billingPage.licenseStripeEnabled: License activates directly and does not depend on Stripe checkout.

invoice.title: Invoice history
invoice.description: Track latest billing periods and collection statuses.
invoice.searchPlaceholder: Search by plan, method, amount, or date...
invoice.empty: No invoice data yet. Invoices will appear after the first successful plan activation.
invoice.noResults: No invoices match the current filters.
invoice.pendingSyncDescription: Invoice feed is waiting for Stripe webhook sync. This section will populate after first successful charge event.
invoice.note: Detailed invoice sync is enabled automatically once Stripe webhooks are configured.

billingPage.ownerOnlyDescription: Only workspace owner/admin or platform admin can manage payment methods and invoices.

webBilling.tagline: Web billing portal for workspace teams
webBilling.landingDescription: Pick a plan on web, pay with Stripe, and sync entitlement back to desktop instantly.
webBilling.tilePricingDescription: Choose workspace plan and start Stripe checkout on web.
webBilling.tilePlansDescription: Review profiles, members, storage, and cycle prices by plan.
webBilling.tileManagementDescription: Track current subscription, invoices, and renewal controls.
webBilling.plansDescription: Reference table for plan limits and public list prices.
webBilling.pricingDescription: Step 1 connect Control API. Step 2 choose workspace and billing cycle. Step 3 continue to Stripe checkout.
webBilling.managementDescription: Monitor subscription status, invoices, and cancellation controls after checkout.
webBilling.connectionDescription: Enter Control API credentials from your workspace so this portal can load team billing data.
webBilling.workspaceSelectionDescription: Select the workspace to bill, then continue with checkout or subscription actions.
webBilling.workspaceRequired: Workspace is required for this action.
webBilling.faq1A: No. Desktop checkout has been fully replaced by this web billing portal.
webBilling.faq2A: The portal uses Control API credentials to load your workspace and apply actions securely.
webBilling.faq3Q: When does entitlement update in desktop?
webBilling.faq3A: After successful checkout, open desktop billing and sync entitlement from web.
webBilling.desktopPricingDescription: Select pricing and start purchase from web. Desktop now syncs entitlement after checkout.
webBilling.desktopManagementTitle: Billing management moved to web portal
webBilling.desktopManagementDescription: Subscription updates, cancellation, and invoice tracking are now web-only.
webBilling.desktopMigrationDescription: In-app checkout flow has been replaced by web billing to reduce local attack surface.
webBilling.desktopManagementMigrationDescription: Use web management for all billing actions, then click sync to refresh desktop entitlement.
webBilling.desktopPortalMissing: Billing portal URL is not configured.
webBilling.desktopContextMissing: Missing sync context. Check sync server URL/token and account info.
webBilling.desktopCheckoutReturnSuccess: Checkout callback received. Open Billing to sync latest entitlement.

footer.title: BugLogin Portal
footer.description: The website layer that owns BugLogin marketing, authentication, checkout, account billing, and internal admin operations.

home.linearSubtitle: Purpose-built for profile isolation, workspace controls, and billing operations.
home.title: Run browser, workspace, and billing operations from one control surface.
home.description: BugLogin combines a desktop antidetect browser with a web control layer for pricing, authentication, checkout, account billing, and super admin workflows without turning the browser app into a billing shell.

2.title: Google OAuth callback healthy
2.subtitle: Portal and desktop share the same authority

surface.accountBillingDescription: Usage, invoices, checkout retries, and entitlement state stay visible on web.

home.capabilitiesTitle: A sales website that still behaves like a real product surface.
home.capabilitiesDescription: Landing, pricing, auth, checkout, account billing, and super admin all sit on top of the same contract used by the desktop product.

stealth.title: The antidetect browser stays at the center
stealth.description: Home, pricing, and auth all sell the actual product instead of collapsing the website into a generic billing microsite.

workspace.title: The portal connects to real workspaces
workspace.description: Plans, invoices, and entitlement states are pulled from the same Control API and database already serving the desktop app.

billing.title: Stripe checkout and account billing stay clean
billing.description: Users handle purchasing, invoices, renewals, and subscription state on web without bloating the desktop runtime.

signin.title: Authenticate through one auth hub
signin.description: Email/password, OTP, Google OAuth, and invite acceptance all converge into a single auth entry point.

checkout.title: Choose a workspace before checkout
checkout.description: The website binds plans to real workspaces before creating Stripe sessions so entitlement never drifts.

operate.title: Operate on web, work in the app
operate.description: Users return to desktop for runtime work while account billing and super admin remain on the website.

home.splitTitle: Public pages stay soft. Logged-in surfaces get denser.
home.splitDescription: The overall brand stays premium and restrained, but logged-in routes shift into a sharper, operations-first app rhythm.

account.title: Real account billing for users
account.description: Users can inspect plans, usage, invoices, and restart checkout when needed.

admin.title: Super admin for internal operations
admin.description: Platform admins can monitor workspace health, revenue signals, audit logs, and system readiness from the same website.

security.q: Is the web portal detached from desktop data?
security.a: No. The portal only reads and writes against the same authority already used by the desktop product, so plan and billing state stay consistent.

billing.a: Public payments run through Stripe checkout. After completion, the portal returns users to account billing to read the updated state.

desktop.q: Do users work on web or desktop after login?
desktop.a: Desktop remains the main product. The website owns marketing, auth, checkout, billing, and super admin surfaces.

pricing.title: Public pricing that is clear on workspace limits and strong enough to convert directly.
pricing.description: The new pricing page sells BugLogin as it is: an antidetect browser for individual operators and teams, paired with cloud sync, billing, and web-based admin control.
pricing.compareHeading: See what each BugLogin plan actually unlocks.
pricing.compareDescription: Review workspace limits, members, storage, proxy quota, and support before sending the user into checkout.
pricing.linearSubtitle: Pick the workspace tier that matches team scale and runtime needs.
pricing.trustedBy: Trusted by product, growth, and anti-detect teams.
pricing.finalCtaTitle: Start with a workspace tier that can actually carry daily browser operations.
pricing.finalCtaDescription: Use the public checkout flow for Starter or Scale, then manage billing, invoices, and plan state from the web portal without going back into the desktop app.

free.name: Free
free.audience: Solo testing and initial profile setup

starter.name: Starter
starter.audience: Small operator teams running a shared workspace

growth.name: Growth
growth.audience: Growing teams that need more profiles, seats, and storage headroom

scale.name: Scale
scale.audience: Production operations with higher concurrency and cloud sync

custom.name: Custom
custom.audience: Advanced teams with custom quota, storage, and billing needs

tableDescriptions.profiles: Maximum isolated browser profiles available in the workspace.
tableDescriptions.members: Team seats that can share billing and workspace access.
tableDescriptions.storage: Cloud storage reserved for profiles, sessions, and sync data.
tableDescriptions.proxy: Included proxy bandwidth quota available inside the plan.
tableDescriptions.support: Support channel and response level included with the tier.

auth.title: One login surface for the BugLogin portal.
auth.description: This flow brings password auth, OTP, Google OAuth, and invite acceptance into a single entry point before checkout or account billing.
auth.googleConfigMissing: Google OAuth is not configured for the portal.
auth.legalHint: By continuing you agree to the
auth.oauthReturn: Returning you to the portal auth hub.

browser.title: Authentication serves the actual product
browser.description: The website is designed to route users into the desktop antidetect browser instead of pretending web is the runtime surface.

billing.title: Checkout can follow immediately after login
billing.description: Pricing and checkout can move directly into a real workspace as soon as the user authenticates.

admin.title: Super admin does not need a detached flow
admin.description: Platform admins share the same auth authority and only diverge by post-auth destination and permissions.

checkout.title: Checkout that binds directly to a real workspace.
checkout.description: Users select a plan, billing cycle, and workspace, then the website creates a Stripe session against the same authority shared with desktop.
checkout.loadWorkspacesFailed: Unable to load workspaces for checkout.
checkout.workspaceRequired: A workspace must be selected before continuing.
checkout.planDescription: The price points and quotas here are shared with the public pricing page and desktop product.
checkout.summaryDescription: The portal only creates a Stripe session after the plan is attached to a real workspace.
checkout.successDescription: The portal is returning you to billing so you can review the latest subscription state.
checkout.cancelDescription: You can return to pricing or relaunch checkout at any time.

account.title: Manage subscriptions, invoices, and usage per workspace.
account.description: The account area keeps plan and payment operations on web while the desktop product stays focused on runtime and profile execution.
account.dashboardDescription: A compact control panel for workspace billing, invoice history, and account-level settings.
account.workspaceHint: Select the workspace whose billing state you want to inspect.
account.workspaceEmpty: No workspace is available in the current portal session.
account.membersDetail: Profile capacity visible to the current billing seat.
account.settingsDescription: Account preferences, workspace-facing defaults, and safety controls stay on web instead of bloating the desktop runtime.
account.securityDescription: Billing access, payment actions, and workspace administration are limited by the current portal role.
account.languageDescription: Website language follows the portal preference so account and admin pages stay consistent with the public web shell.
account.notificationsDescription: Billing and invoice updates should stay on web, while the desktop app remains focused on runtime work.
account.subscriptionDescription: The portal lets users change plans, relaunch checkout, and control the subscription state of the selected workspace.
account.invoiceDescription: Recent invoices are rendered directly from the existing billing state endpoints.
account.invoiceEmpty: No invoices were found for this workspace.
account.paymentMethodDescription: Funding sources are managed through Stripe-hosted checkout. When users need to update payment details, the portal launches a secure checkout flow for the active workspace.
account.checkoutSuccessDescription: The portal has returned to billing. If the webhook has finished processing, the updated plan should already be visible here.
account.checkoutCancelDescription: The current plan was not changed. You can relaunch checkout at any time.

overview.title: Platform overview
overview.description: One control surface for workspace risk, revenue motion, and service readiness across the platform.
overview.workspacesDescription: Total workspaces currently visible through the control plane.
overview.activeDescription: Subscriptions in an active state and available for production runtime.
overview.riskDescription: Workspaces that require operator attention because billing is degraded.
overview.auditDescription: High-touch workspaces that currently rely on custom or manually managed plans.

commandCenter.title: Command center for the full control plane.
commandCenter.description: A high-level cockpit for platform admins to read workspace state, operational queue pressure, and service readiness at a glance.
commandCenter.queueDescription: Workspaces that need attention are grouped by risk, billing, and subscription status signals.
commandCenter.systemDescription: A concise read on auth, Stripe, and sync availability.

workspaces.title: Workspaces across the system.
workspaces.description: This view gives platform admins a fast way to inspect workspace health, limits, and usage in one place.
workspaces.tableDescription: Data is pulled directly from the existing admin workspace health endpoint.

revenue.title: Revenue signals and entitlement state.
revenue.description: The website exposes the revenue-facing signals that already exist on the control plane without forcing that reporting into the desktop app.
revenue.latestDescription: A quick list of workspaces with the freshest invoice timestamps to track renewal cadence and revenue motion.

audit.title: Audit trail for platform actions.
audit.description: Critical admin actions can be reviewed directly on the website instead of forcing operators through disconnected internal tooling.
audit.tableDescription: Recent records from the control plane are rendered directly in-table.

system.title: System readiness and runtime configuration.
system.description: This route focuses on control plane connectivity, token readiness, and the health of the core services the portal depends on.
system.runtimeDescription: The portal shares the same runtime contract as desktop, but reads it in a website-specific context.
system.operationsDescription: Short reminders for checking auth, billing, and sync when state appears inconsistent.
system.hintAuthDescription: If login or registration breaks, validate the public auth endpoints and token configuration on the sync runtime first.
system.hintStripeDescription: Public checkout should only be exposed when both the Stripe secret and webhook are fully ready.
system.hintSyncDescription: If usage or entitlement appears out of sync, verify the sync storage and control state pipeline first.

help.title: Fast support for the new BugLogin website
help.description: This page explains where the website fits in the product and when users should return to the desktop app.

download.title: Download and run the desktop app
download.description: The website handles conversion and billing, but the desktop application remains the main place where browser profiles and workspace operations run.

billing.title: Billing is now fully web-based
billing.description: Plan purchases, checkout relaunches, invoices, and subscription state management all happen in the new portal.

admin.title: Super Admin now lives on the website
admin.description: Platform admins can open command center, workspace health, revenue, and audit routes directly on the single-domain portal.

security.title: The website shares the same authority as desktop
security.description: The portal does not create a parallel database or API. It reads and writes against the same authority used for auth, billing, and workspace control.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.description: The new BugLogin website is the front door for conversion, billing, and admin operations, so the terms focus on workspace access, governance, and acceptable use.

1.title: Usage scope
1.body: BugLogin accounts are provisioned for individuals or teams that have registered for the service. Every billing, auth, and admin action on the portal is tied back to a workspace and audit trail.

2.title: Governance and access
2.body: Platform admins, workspace owners, and workspace admins may only perform actions that are explicitly allowed by the active control plane permissions.

3.title: Payment compliance
3.body: Public purchases are processed through Stripe. Final payment state is determined by the billing authority and valid webhook processing.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: The new portal only collects and renders the data necessary for authentication, billing, and admin operations.

1.title: Account data
1.body: Email, user identifiers, avatars, and platform roles are used to authenticate users and route access correctly across the website.

2.title: Billing data
2.body: Workspaces, subscriptions, invoices, and usage metrics are synchronized from the billing and control plane systems into account billing and super admin.

3.title: Operational logs
3.body: Administrative actions may appear in audit logs to maintain traceability and operational safety for the platform.

refund.title: Refund policy
refund.description: Refunds are handled according to the active subscription state and the payment rules currently enforced by BugLogin.

1.title: Refund requests
1.body: Each request should be tied to a valid workspace, invoice, or checkout session so the team can verify the billing state accurately.

2.title: Processing window
2.body: The portal exposes invoice history and current plan state, while final refund handling follows the billing workflow defined by the system.

3.title: Impact on entitlement
3.body: After a refund or plan cancellation, the workspace entitlement may move according to the active, grace, or read-only policy.
